isolation_opt_out_reason: Bundled multi-feature post-merge closure in chore/v7-9-1-close-stuck-features — all 4 features were v7.9.1 build-window ships (PRs #625-#628) that landed yesterday and stuck at implementation phase due to FEATURE_CLOSURE_COMPLETENESS pr_list_parity_mismatch firing pre-PR-list-citation; chore bundling reduces PR overhead vs 4 separate post-merge-closure branches per close-feature.py default pattern
primary_metric: FT2 surfaces iOS Slather + Python pytest-cov coverage via `make coverage-report` + .github/workflows/coverage.yml warn-only CI baseline
success_metrics: make coverage-ios target exists, skips cleanly when slather not installed, runs slather coverage against the most recent .xcresult when available (T1, measured by smoke-test)
make coverage-py target exists, skips cleanly when pytest-cov not installed, runs pytest --cov in ai-engine/ when available (T1, measured by smoke-test)
make coverage-report meta-target runs both surfaces and prints a per-target line-rate summary (T1, measured by smoke-test)
.github/workflows/coverage.yml CI workflow exists with two jobs (coverage-ios on macos-15, coverage-py on ubuntu-latest), both continue-on-error: true (T1, measured by yaml lint + presence)
Coverage telemetry begins accumulating against which v8.0 GATE_TEST_MISSING meta-gate (backlog T1) can calibrate per-module thresholds (T2, narrative — depends on 30+ days CI runs)
kill_criteria: Coverage workflow flakes on a single iOS test and gates PRs — mitigated by continue-on-error: true on every job
Slather aggregation breaks on a future Xcode version — mitigated by skip-cleanly-if-absent in the Makefile target; workflow falls back to artifact-only upload
Coverage output bloats CI logs — mitigated by `| tail -30` on the pytest output + artifact upload for full XML
kill_criteria_resolution: All 3 mitigated by design. (1) continue-on-error: true on all 3 jobs (build, slather-aggregate, pytest-cov) means a flake produces a `failure` annotation in the run logs but the PR-level check still resolves to success. (2) The Makefile target has explicit `command -v slather` and ls-of-xcresult guards; missing tool prints a skip notice and exits 0. (3) The ai-engine pytest output is piped through `tail -30` for the summary and the full coverage.xml is uploaded as an artifact for 14-day retention.
dispatch_pattern: agent-driven (Makefile edit + new workflow YAML + state.json)
scope_summary: R9 Track B (post-Phase-E hygiene). Track A shipped 2026-05-25 (.slather.yml + [tool.coverage.*]). This PR adds the operator surface — 3 Makefile targets (coverage-ios, coverage-py, coverage-report) + 1 CI workflow (.github/workflows/coverage.yml) with two warn-only jobs (iOS Slather on macos-15, Python pytest-cov on ubuntu-latest). Coverage data flows into upload-artifact for 14-day retention so the v8.0 GATE_TEST_MISSING meta-gate (backlog T1) has data to calibrate against. Web c8 is intentionally out of scope (lives in fitme-story per dev-env-plan).

tasks.description: Add coverage-ios / coverage-py / coverage-report targets to Makefile with skip-cleanly-if-absent guards
Create .github/workflows/coverage.yml with macos-15 iOS Slather job + ubuntu-latest pytest-cov job, both continue-on-error: true
Smoke-test: make coverage-py + make coverage-ios skip cleanly on a machine without pytest-cov/slather
Update docs/master-plan/dev-env-master-plan-2026-05-24.md R9 row: [~] Track A SHIPPED → [x] Track A + Track B SHIPPED

implementation.notes: Track B operator surface for R9. No gate; warn-only baseline; data feeds v8.0 GATE_TEST_MISSING meta-gate calibration.
